critiques.pain_point: No urgency indicators on the homepage for last-minute bookings
critiques.reason: Users under time pressure need critical actions surfaced immediately. The homepage does not prioritize urgent booking flows, increasing cognitive load.

critiques.pain_point: Search results load slowly, increasing user stress
critiques.reason: Slow feedback violates Nielsen's visibility of system status heuristic. Users under time pressure will abandon if they cannot see progress.

critiques.pain_point: No filter for departure time within the next 2 hours
critiques.reason: Lack of relevant filtering forces users to scan irrelevant results, increasing cognitive load and frustration during an urgent task.

critiques.pain_point: Forced account creation before completing booking
critiques.reason: Mandatory registration is a known friction point that causes drop-offs. Baymard research identifies it as a top reason for checkout abandonment.

critiques.pain_point: Unclear error message on credit card field
critiques.reason: Vague error messages violate Nielsen's error message heuristic. Users cannot recover efficiently without knowing exactly what went wrong.

critiques.pain_point: App provides no filter for part-time or flexible hours
critiques.reason: Without relevant filters, users must manually scan all listings. This creates unnecessary cognitive load and poor findability for a specific user need.

critiques.pain_point: Job listings are dense with no clear visual hierarchy
critiques.reason: Poor visual hierarchy on a small screen forces users to read every listing rather than scan. This violates mobile usability guidelines for information density.

critiques.pain_point: Page load time is too slow on campus wifi
critiques.reason: Slow load times on mobile violate system status feedback principles. Users on intermittent connections need lightweight pages and loading indicators.

critiques.pain_point: Redirecting to an external site for application breaks the flow
critiques.reason: Sending users to a third-party site mid-task creates a jarring context switch. This friction point often causes abandonment before application completion.

critiques.pain_point: Navigation menu has too many options causing decision paralysis
critiques.reason: Hick's Law states that more choices increases decision time. For elderly first-time users, an overwhelming nav menu significantly raises cognitive load.

critiques.pain_point: No clear back button visible after wrong navigation
critiques.reason: Nielsen's heuristic for user control requires clear undo and back options. Trapping users on the wrong page causes frustration and loss of confidence.

critiques.pain_point: Search returns unhelpful results for appointment scheduling
critiques.reason: Poor search relevance forces users to browse manually. For elderly users unfamiliar with the system, failed search is a critical failure point.

critiques.pain_point: Form uses small font and unclear required field indicators
critiques.reason: Small text and ambiguous required fields violate accessibility guidelines. WCAG requires sufficient contrast and clear labeling for form inputs.

critiques.pain_point: Confirmation message disappears too quickly
critiques.reason: Transient feedback without persistence leaves users uncertain whether their action succeeded. This violates system status visibility and causes repeat submissions.

critiques.pain_point: No indication of minimum order amount before checkout
critiques.reason: Hiding constraints until checkout is a deceptive pattern that damages trust. Users should know order requirements upfront to avoid wasted effort.

critiques.pain_point: Search does not return brand-specific results
critiques.reason: Poor search relevance forces users to browse manually. When users have a specific item in mind, failed search directly causes task abandonment.

critiques.pain_point: Sponsored product suggestions distract from the user's task
critiques.reason: Interrupting a focused task flow with promotions increases cognitive load and diverts attention. This is especially disruptive for time-pressured users.

critiques.pain_point: Delivery time slots unavailable with no earlier warning
critiques.reason: Revealing slot unavailability only at checkout wastes user effort and causes frustration. Availability should be surfaced at the start of the flow.

critiques.pain_point: Invoice template options are unlabeled and visually unclear
critiques.reason: Unlabeled UI elements violate recognition over recall principles. Users should not need to guess what each template does before selecting one.

critiques.pain_point: Tax field behavior is ambiguous with no inline explanation
critiques.reason: Ambiguous form fields cause errors and slow task completion. Inline help text or tooltips would reduce uncertainty without disrupting the flow.

critiques.pain_point: Invoice preview does not match the final PDF output
critiques.reason: Inconsistency between preview and output violates the match between system and real world heuristic, eroding user trust in the tool.

critiques.pain_point: No confirmation that the invoice email was delivered
critiques.reason: Lack of delivery feedback violates system status visibility. Users need confirmation that a business-critical action completed successfully.

critiques.pain_point: No sent history visible in the invoicing tool
critiques.reason: Without a sent history, users cannot verify past actions or recover from errors. This creates anxiety around business-critical communications.
